summary: Iteration 5 (belly_cm + /api/measurements/latest) fully validated. Backend pytest suite extended 50 → 55 tests (+5 new tests in TestBellyAndLatest). 55/55 PASS in 32.26s. New coverage: (1) POST /api/measurements accepts belly_cm and GET /api/measurements returns it; (2) GET /api/measurements/latest returns all 7 fields as null for a fresh user; (3) with 3 backdated mixed measurements /latest picks the most recent non-null per field independently (weight from newest, chest from middle overrides oldest, waist from oldest, belly from middle, arm from newest, hips/thigh remain null); (4) legacy documents inserted without belly_cm key deserialize gracefully to belly_cm=null (no 500); (5) seed user test@bp.com /latest response shape validated (all 7 keys present, each either null or {value, created_at}). Regression: all 50 tests from iterations 1-4 remain green (auth, workouts CRUD/AI, meals CRUD/AI suggest, coach chat, profile Mifflin-St Jeor, adaptive calorie reco + apply + cooldown + bounds).

minor.issue: Still returns 403 (HTTPBearer default) instead of 401 – carried over from iter 1, non-blocking.

action_items: (LOW) Carried over from iter 4: server.py is now ~1050 lines — consider splitting into modules (auth, profile, workouts, meals, measurements, coach, adaptive) for maintainability.
(LOW) Carried over: return 401 instead of 403 when Authorization header missing (customize HTTPBearer).
critical_code_review_comments: Measurement model (server.py:185-196) correctly adds belly_cm: Optional[float] = None with default None — legacy documents missing the field deserialize cleanly (verified by test_legacy_measurement_without_belly_cm_deserializes).
MeasurementCreate (server.py:199-207) mirrors the model with belly_cm as Optional[float] = None — payloads without belly_cm still succeed.

GET /api/measurements/latest (server.py:557-573) iterates docs sorted DESC by created_at and breaks on first non-null per field — correct semantics for 'most recent non-null'. Returns {value, created_at} or null cleanly. Fields list hardcoded to 7 body parts matches the spec.

The 'break' inside the inner for-loop plus the 'if f not in result' fallback (server.py:571-572) correctly handles the case where a field is never set → null. Verified by test_latest_all_null_for_new_user and hips/thigh null branch in mixed test.
No new endpoint uses MongoDB _id in responses — {"_id": 0} projection preserved (server.py:562). Good.
Adaptive calorie logic (_maybe_apply_adaptive_calories) still triggers on POST /api/measurements when weight_kg is provided — belly_cm-only POSTs correctly skip the adaptive path (server.py:581).
seed_data_creation: 5 new TEST_iter5_* users registered via /api/auth/register, plus a handful of measurements inserted directly into MongoDB (note='TEST_iter5_old'/'_mid'/'_new'/'_legacy'). No cleanup performed (matches previous iteration pattern). Seed user test@bp.com untouched by iter5 tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 (belly_cm + /api/measurements/latest) fully green. Test file /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py now has 55 tests. New TestBellyAndLatest class (5 tests) covers belly_cm CRUD, /latest empty case, /latest mixed-data-most-recent-per-field, legacy doc backward compat, and seed user /latest shape. Endpoints exercised: POST /api/measurements (belly_cm), GET /api/measurements (belly_cm field present), GET /api/measurements/latest (new endpoint). Reuses helpers _register_user, _h, _set_profile from iter 4. Direct pymongo inserts used to backdate created_at and to insert legacy-shape docs without belly_cm key.
